reason: All FR-20 acceptance criteria are met. EscalationRequest is a frozen dataclass with conversation_id (int), reason (EscalationReason), and priority (int 0/1/2). EscalationReason enum exposes out_of_scope, low_confidence, emotion_trigger as string values. SLA_BY_PRIORITY = {0:30, 1:15, 2:5} is a module-level dict (configurable). create() validates priority, computes sla_deadline = datetime.now(UTC)+timedelta(minutes=sla_minutes), inserts into escalation_queue with RETURNING id. assign() sets assigned_agent + picked_at=NOW() and raises KeyError if not found. resolve() sets resolved_at=NOW() with same guard. get_sla_breaches() correctly filters resolved_at IS NULL AND sla_deadline < NOW(), ordering priority DESC, created_at ASC. One minor naming note: SRS says 'queued_at ASC' but implementation uses 'created_at ASC' — functionally equivalent assuming the column is the insertion timestamp; not a defect. No regressions detected.
gaps: get_sla_breaches ORDER BY uses created_at instead of queued_at (SRS wording); verify column name in DB schema to confirm equivalence
